rationale: The correct answer is 285. The number of people predicted to visit the beach each day is represented by the y-values of the line of best fit, and the average temperature, in degrees Celsius (), is represented by the x-values. Since the slope of the line of best fit is approximately 57, the y-value, or the number of people predicted to visit the beach each day, increases by 57 for every x-value increase of 1, or every increase in average temperature. Therefore, an increase of in average temperature corresponds to a y-value increase of additional people per day predicted to visit the beach.
